name: Niger

colors.symbolism: the Sahara desert in the north of the country and the savannah
purity and innocence; civic spirit; the Niger River
the fertile southern lands along the Niger River and hope

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — orange, white and green — with an orange disc centred on the white middle stripe. Aspect ratio approximately 6:7 (close to square), unusually tall for a national flag.
history: Adopted on 23 November 1959, ahead of Niger's independence from France on 3 August 1960. The flag was retained at independence and through subsequent constitutional changes. The unusually near-square 6:7 proportions are a distinctive feature, though in practice the flag is often produced in 2:3 or other rectangular ratios for export.

features.symbolism: orange for the Sahara desert that covers the north, white for purity and the Niger River, green for the fertile southern lands and hope
the sun, and the willingness of the people to defend their independence; the sacrifices of the people
